criteria.criterion: OS detection returns correct OS family for known distributions via /etc/os-release
criteria.evidence: detector.py:114-147 - _detect_from_os_release() reads /etc/os-release and looks up in registry

criteria.criterion: Config file overrides dynamic detection when OS is explicitly specified
criteria.evidence: detector.py:68-78 - set_explicit_os() returns source='config' with confidence=1.0

criteria.criterion: Unknown distribution triggers auto-discovery and registers with detected info
criteria.evidence: detector.py:129-147 - Auto-registers unknown distros with _infer_family() and _infer_package_manager()

criteria.criterion: Registry lookup by distro ID returns correct OSInfo
criteria.evidence: registry.py:38-48 - lookup() returns OSInfo from _registry dict

criteria.criterion: os_cmd('info') returns structured system info on any registered OS
criteria.evidence: commands.py:93-110 + linux.py:77-108 - os_cmd('info') returns JSON with os, hostname, kernel, architecture

criteria.criterion: Command adapter registry correctly routes to OS-specific implementation
criteria.evidence: adapter.py:170-181 - get_adapter() returns adapter for current OS from _adapters dict

criteria.criterion: YAML config can override default command mappings for any OS
criteria.evidence: commands.yaml:7-51 - command_overrides maps OS-specific commands; adapter.py:183-191 - load_command_overrides() stores overrides

criteria.criterion: Unregistered OS falls back to generic Linux adapter
criteria.evidence: adapter.py:150-158 + 179-180 - set_generic_adapter() sets fallback; get_adapter() returns generic if OS not found

truths.claim: OS识别机制采用混合模式: 配置文件为主，动态探测为辅
truths.evidence: detector.py:68-78 (config primary, confidence 1.0) vs detector.py:80-98 (dynamic detection secondary)

truths.claim: 测试用例抽象层级为场景层抽象: YAML/JSON配置驱动
truths.evidence: commands.yaml drives command mappings; commands.py:os_cmd() is unified interface

truths.claim: OS特定适配扩展点为命令适配层: 统一命令接口
truths.evidence: CommandAdapter ABC with execute(cmd_name, **kwargs); os_cmd('info') unified interface
